ksa_text: Knowledge of data and AI maturity assessment frameworks and how to diagnose organizational capability gaps across strategy, governance, architecture, talent, and culture dimensions.

dcwf.top_element_label: Demonstrate to executive stakeholders how data and analytics initiatives address agency challenges.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
Demonstrate to executive stakeholders how data and analytics initiatives address agency challenges.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Leadership & Strategy is a primary or secondary domain for the Chief of Staff to CDAIO. This knowledge KSA is within scope for the role's expected competency profile and seniority level (senior specialist).

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (Leadership & Strategy), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.517). External reviewers may question whether this specific knowledge KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the Chief of Staff to CDAIO versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: McKinsey, Gartner, and Harvard Business Review consistently identify leadership and strategy skills as core differentiators for C-suite and VP data leaders.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: moderate framework signal is consistent with Leadership & Strategy appearing in job postings and role definition literature for Chief of Staff to CDAIO.

decision_rationale: Leadership & Strategy knowledge KSA is within scope for the Chief of Staff to CDAIO at the senior specialist tier. Seniority fit is appropriate. All three evaluation passes support inclusion.
